name: PB-10 | Cloud Misconfiguration Triage & Auto-Remediation
description: Production playbook. Ingests cloud misconfiguration alerts from AWS GuardDuty, AWS CloudTrail, and Azure Defender via Wazuh. Enriches findings with AWS Config, IAM context, resource tagging, and exposure assessment. Auto-remediates high-confidence misconfigurations (revoke public S3 ACL, close overly permissive security groups, revoke IAM over-permissions) or creates structured Jira remediation tickets with full context for analyst-driven remediation. Final playbook in the 10-playbook Autonomous IR Pipeline.

trigger.description: Listens for Wazuh cloud misconfiguration alerts from AWS CloudTrail/GuardDuty and Azure Defender on rule group: cloud,pb10 at level >= 13

nodes.name: Webhook Trigger
nodes.description: Entry point. Receives cloud misconfiguration alert from Wazuh — AWS CloudTrail, GuardDuty, or Azure Defender.

nodes.name: Artifact Extraction & Provider Classification
nodes.description: Normalizes cloud alert fields across AWS and Azure sources. Classifies misconfiguration type and pre-flags zero-tolerance conditions — root account usage and public S3 buckets with sensitive data tags.

nodes.name: Critical Misconfiguration Fast-Track Gate
nodes.description: Root account usage and public S3 buckets bypass full enrichment and trigger immediate remediation. Both represent zero-tolerance conditions — root account should never be used operationally, and a public S3 bucket is an active data exposure event.

nodes.name: AWS Config / Azure Resource — Asset Context
nodes.description: Queries AWS Config or Azure Resource Manager for full resource metadata — tags (including data classification, owner, environment), creation date, compliance state, and related resource configuration. Tags drive the severity and SLA of the remediation ticket.

output_variables.is_sensitive_data: {{ $result.configurationItems[0].tags.DataClassification | default('') | contains('Confidential') or $result.configurationItems[0].tags.DataClassification | default('') | contains('PII') or $result.configurationItems[0].tags.DataClassification | default('') | contains('PHI') }}

nodes.name: AWS IAM / Azure AD — Actor Identity Context
nodes.description: Queries IAM to get full context on who made the misconfiguration — their role, attached policies, group memberships, and MFA status. Determines whether this was an authorized change, an accident, or a compromised/malicious actor.

nodes.name: Network Exposure Assessment
nodes.description: Determines the blast radius of the misconfiguration. Public S3 with PII data = maximum exposure. Open security group on RDP port 3389 on production DB server = critical. Combines resource sensitivity, environment classification, and misconfiguration type.

nodes.name: AWS Config / Azure — Scan for Similar Misconfigurations
nodes.description: Queries AWS Config Rules or Azure Policy to find other resources with the same type of misconfiguration. A single open security group might be an accident. Ten open security groups is a systemic problem. Scope drives the remediation priority.

input.body: {{ $node_02_extract.cloud_provider == 'AWS' ? '{"ConfigRuleName": "' + $node_02_extract.misconfig_type | lower | replace('_', '-') + '"}' : '{"$filter": "ComplianceState eq NonCompliant and PolicyDefinitionId eq \'' + $node_02_extract.misconfig_type + '\'"}'  }}

nodes.name: Misconfiguration Risk Score
nodes.description: Combines exposure score with systemic spread and GuardDuty severity into final risk score. Systemic misconfigurations across multiple resources get elevated — they indicate a process failure, not a one-off accident.

nodes.name: Auto-Remediation Decision Gate
nodes.description: Routes to auto-remediation if score >= 65. Lower-confidence findings (non-production resources, no sensitive data tags, low GuardDuty severity) route to Jira ticket creation only — analyst decides on remediation approach.

nodes.name: Auto-Remediation — Revoke Misconfiguration
nodes.description: Executes the appropriate AWS/Azure API call to remediate the misconfiguration. Action depends on misconfig_type: PUBLIC_S3 → set bucket ACL to private, OPEN_SECURITY_GROUP → revoke 0.0.0.0/0 ingress rule, ROOT_ACCOUNT → revoke root access keys, IAM_ESCALATION → detach over-privileged policy.

output_variables.remediation_action: {{ $node_02_extract.misconfig_type == 'PUBLIC_S3' ? 'S3 bucket ACL set to private' : $node_02_extract.misconfig_type == 'OPEN_SECURITY_GROUP' ? 'Security group ingress rule 0.0.0.0/0 revoked' : $node_02_extract.misconfig_type == 'IAM_ESCALATION' ? 'Over-privileged IAM policy detached' : 'Root access key deleted' }}

nodes.name: Jira — Create Cloud Remediation Ticket
nodes.description: Creates a structured Jira cloud security remediation ticket. Assigns to the resource owner identified from tags. Includes full context, exposure assessment, similar misconfiguration count, and step-by-step remediation instructions.

nodes.name: TheHive — Create Case for Critical Cloud Findings
nodes.description: Creates a TheHive case only for critical-exposure cloud findings — production resources with sensitive data, root account usage, and GuardDuty high-severity findings. Routine misconfigurations on non-production resources are tracked in Jira only.

input.title: PB-10 | Cloud {{ $node_06_exposure_assessment.exposure_level }} — {{ $node_02_extract.misconfig_type | replace('_', ' ') }} | {{ $node_04_resource_context.resource_name }} | Score: {{ $node_08_score.composite_score }}
input.description:
## Cloud Misconfiguration — Critical Finding

**Alert ID:** {{ $exec.alert_id }}
**Timestamp:** {{ $exec.timestamp }}
**Provider:** {{ $node_02_extract.cloud_provider }}
**Misconfiguration:** {{ $node_02_extract.misconfig_type }}
**Exposure Level:** {{ $node_06_exposure_assessment.exposure_level }}
**Risk Score:** {{ $node_08_score.composite_score }} / 100
**Auto-Remediated:** {{ $node_10_auto_remediate.remediation_action | default('No — see Jira ticket') }}
**Jira Ticket:** {{ $node_11_create_jira.jira_ticket_url }}

### Affected Resource
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Provider | {{ $node_02_extract.cloud_provider }} |
| Account/Subscription | {{ $node_02_extract.account_id }} |
| Region | {{ $node_02_extract.region }} |
| Resource Type | {{ $node_02_extract.resource_type }} |
| Resource ID | {{ $node_02_extract.resource_id }} |
| Resource Name | {{ $node_04_resource_context.resource_name }} |
| Environment | {{ $node_04_resource_context.resource_env }} |
| Owner | {{ $node_04_resource_context.resource_owner }} |
| Data Classification | {{ $node_04_resource_context.resource_data_class }} |
| Production | {{ $node_04_resource_context.is_production }} |
| Sensitive Data Tags | {{ $node_04_resource_context.is_sensitive_data }} |

### Actor
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Username | {{ $node_02_extract.actor_username }} |
| Actor Type | {{ $node_02_extract.actor_type }} |
| ARN | {{ $node_02_extract.actor_arn }} |
| Source IP | {{ $node_02_extract.source_ip }} |
| MFA Enabled | {{ $node_05_iam_context.actor_mfa_enabled }} |
| Attached Policies | {{ $node_05_iam_context.actor_policies | join(', ') }} |

### Exposure Assessment
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Exposure Score | {{ $node_06_exposure_assessment.exposure_score }} |
| Exposure Level | {{ $node_06_exposure_assessment.exposure_level }} |
| GuardDuty Severity | {{ $node_02_extract.finding_severity }} |

### Scope
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Similar Misconfigurations | {{ $node_07_similar_misconfigs.similar_misconfig_count }} |
| Systemic Issue | {{ $node_07_similar_misconfigs.is_systemic }} |
| Other Affected Resources | {{ $node_07_similar_misconfigs.similar_resources | join(', ') }} |

### Auto-Remediation
| Action | Status |
|---|---|
| Remediation Action | {{ $node_10_auto_remediate.remediation_action | default('Not auto-remediated') }} |
| Status | {{ $node_10_auto_remediate.remediation_status | default('Pending manual remediation') }} |

### Investigation Checklist
- [ ] Confirm auto-remediation applied correctly — verify in AWS/Azure console
- [ ] Contact actor {{ $node_02_extract.actor_username }} to determine if change was intentional
- [ ] If GuardDuty finding — review full finding detail in GuardDuty console for threat context
- [ ] Check CloudTrail/Activity Log for other actions by the same actor in the past 24 hours
- [ ] If root account used — verify root access keys are deleted and root MFA is enabled
- [ ] Review all {{ $node_07_similar_misconfigs.similar_misconfig_count }} similar misconfigurations for systemic remediation
- [ ] Enable AWS Config rule / Azure Policy to prevent recurrence of this misconfiguration type
- [ ] Review resource data classification tags — ensure sensitive buckets/DBs are correctly tagged
- [ ] Verify Jira remediation ticket assigned to correct owner: {{ $node_04_resource_context.resource_owner }}

nodes.name: Slack — Cloud Security Alert
nodes.description: Posts structured cloud security alert to the cloud security channel. Root account and critical exposure findings get elevated messaging.

payload.text: *PB-10 | Cloud {{ $node_06_exposure_assessment.exposure_level }} Finding{{ $node_02_extract.is_root == true ? ' — :rotating_light: ROOT ACCOUNT USED' : '' }}{{ $node_04_resource_context.is_sensitive_data == true ? ' — SENSITIVE DATA EXPOSED' : '' }}*

metrics.estimated_mttd_reduction: Real-time — CloudTrail events stream to Wazuh and webhook immediately on API call
metrics.estimated_mttr_reduction: S3 ACL revoked, SG rule removed in < 5 seconds for fast-track path vs. hours of manual remediation
metrics.key_differentiator: Resource tag-driven severity — DataClassification and Environment tags gate auto-remediation vs. Jira-only response
